content: Stripe does not sign a BAA, so PHI must never enter customer metadata, product/price names, line-item descriptors, or invoice descriptions. Treat this as a policy enforced by `docs/stripe-data-policy.md` + a grep/regex CI check, not as "we'll be careful." The bug class (condition names leaking through descriptors) is silent and only surfaces during audits or breach review.
context: Architecture decision in the Apoth telehealth plan (2026-05-19). All other vendors in the BAA boundary can hold PHI; Stripe alone cannot. The fix shape is: opaque internal codes (`tx-cond-a-monthly`) in Stripe, human-readable names rendered from our DB at display time. Codified in T-023 (Stripe SDK setup + BAA-aware metadata policy).
title: Codify "no PHI in Stripe" as policy + lint, not just convention
